subagent_reports.topic: Dependabot as first-class equal path vs Renovate for strict-off + squash-only + linear-history + native auto-merge + required 'Sharperflow CI Gate'

sources.label: Automating Dependabot with GitHub Actions (official)
sources.summary: Canonical auto-merge pattern: dependabot/fetch-metadata + gh pr merge --auto gated on dependabot[bot]; requires contents:write + pull-requests:write; NOTE block explicitly recommends 'Require status checks to pass before merging' so PRs are not merged unless required checks pass.

sources.label: dependabot/fetch-metadata README
sources.summary: Auto-merge enables PRs to merge once CI and branch protection rules are met; pull_request_target variant gives GITHUB_TOKEN read/write with contents:write + pull-requests:write.

sources.label: Dependabot options reference — cooldown
sources.summary: cooldown is GA: default-days, semver-major/minor/patch-days, include/exclude (<=150 each, wildcards). Version updates ONLY — explicitly NOT security updates. 1-90 day range. SemVer-day granularity table: Bun/UV/npm supported; Docker, Docker Compose, github-actions NOT semver-supported (default-days only).

sources.label: Dependabot supported ecosystems and repositories
sources.summary: uv=`uv` v0; Bun=`bun` >=v1.2.5 (docs main; one cached page said >=v1.1.39); pnpm uses `npm` value, version updates v7-v10, security updates v7/v8 only; Docker=`docker` v1; github-actions Not applicable version. GitHub Actions only supports GitHub-repo action syntax; not local refs; not docker:// actions.

sources.label: GitHub Changelog — Dependabot updates SHA version comments
sources.summary: Dependabot DOES pin GitHub Actions to commit SHA and update the trailing semver comment (e.g. `@sha # v4.0.0`). Known edge-case bugs exist (dependabot-core #8011, #14716) where comment is left stale for release-branch-workflow actions.

sources.label: About Dependabot security updates
sources.summary: Security updates open PRs to minimum patched version for every open Dependabot alert with a fix; require dependency graph + Dependabot alerts enabled. Independent of version-update config; cooldown does NOT apply to security updates.

subagent_reports.architecture_assessment: Dependabot is a viable first-class equal path to Renovate for THIS exact merge strategy. (a) Auto-merge: works via the canonical fetch-metadata + `gh pr merge --auto` workflow; --auto arms GitHub native auto-merge, which merges only when the required check 'Sharperflow CI Gate' is green — same gate Renovate relies on. Squash is selected by `gh pr merge --squash` (not --merge), satisfying squash-only + linear history. (b) strict-off: with strict_required_status_checks_policy=false, neither updater is forced to rebase to branch HEAD; both avoid the 'require branches up to date' rebase loop identically. (c) Coexistence: docs are clear each ecosystem should have ONE updater — running Dependabot AND Renovate on the same package-ecosystem in the same repo produces duplicate PRs and competing lockfile writes. They can coexist only if partitioned (different repos, or strictly non-overlapping ecosystems), which is why per-repo selection is the right model. (d) Cooldown parity: Dependabot now HAS a `cooldown` option (GA), the direct analog of Renovate `minimumReleaseAge`, with finer SemVer-tier granularity — BUT version-updates only and NO semver-tier granularity for Docker/github-actions (default-days only there). (e) Ecosystem gaps vs Renovate: Bun and uv are supported but newer/v0/min-version-gated; github-actions SHA-pinning + comment maintenance exists but has documented stale-comment edge cases; Renovate's helpers:pinGitHubActionDigests is more battle-tested for digest pinning at scale.

validation.notes: All 6 questions doc-verified against docs.github.com / github.blog. One UNKNOWN-resolved: cooldown is CONFIRMED GA (not speculative). Two items flagged as caveats not blockers: (1) Bun min-version differs between cached pages (>=v1.1.39 vs >=v1.2.5 on docs main) — treat >=v1.2.5 as current; (2) github-actions SHA-comment maintenance has open upstream bugs. PACKET DEFECT: orchestrator packet omitted SCOPE KEY/ATTEMPT/CHANGE/DONE_WHEN/STOP_WHEN/VERIFICATION anchors; continued under existing prompt scope per instructions and did not infer identity heuristically beyond the reachable active change id.

subagent_reports.recommendation: Adopt balanced dual-path guidance: BOTH Renovate and Dependabot are compatible with strict-off + squash-only + linear-history + native auto-merge gated on 'Sharperflow CI Gate'. Enforce ONE updater per repo (per-ecosystem) to avoid duplicate-PR/lockfile collisions. For the Dependabot path ship the canonical auto-merge workflow with `--squash` (not --merge), contents:write + pull-requests:write, dependabot[bot] actor guard, and keep 'Sharperflow CI Gate' as a required status check (the doc-recommended only-when-green guard). Set `cooldown.default-days: 7` to match Renovate minimumReleaseAge; document that cooldown does not gate security updates in either tool. For the future per-repo re-assessment, weight: Bun/uv maturity (Dependabot v0/min-version gated), github-actions digest-pinning robustness (Renovate helpers:pinGitHubActionDigests stronger; Dependabot has stale-comment edge cases), grouping/config ergonomics, and cooldown granularity needs.
follow_ups: PACKET DEFECT: missing SCOPE KEY/ATTEMPT/CHANGE/DONE_WHEN/STOP_WHEN/VERIFICATION anchors in orchestrator packet — supply on next dispatch.
Verify current Bun minimum version live before publishing guidance (cached pages disagree: >=v1.1.39 vs >=v1.2.5).
If github-actions digest pinning is critical, prefer Renovate helpers:pinGitHubActionDigests or monitor dependabot-core #8011/#14716 for stale-comment fixes.

subagent_reports.topic: Enforcing squash-only merges via org-level GitHub repository ruleset

sources.label: GitHub Docs — Available rules for rulesets (Require a pull request before merging)
sources.summary: Documents that the 'Require a pull request before merging' rule optionally lets you require a merge type of merge, squash, or rebase. 'Additionally if the repository has disabled a merge method and the ruleset required a different method, the merge will be blocked.' Also: Require linear history forbids merge commits, meaning PRs must use squash OR rebase (does NOT distinguish squash from rebase).

sources.label: GitHub Docs — REST API endpoints for rules (repos/rules)
sources.summary: pull_request rule type parameters include allowed_merge_methods (array of strings). 'Array of allowed merge methods. Allowed values include merge, squash, and rebase. At least one option must be enabled.' Supported values: merge, squash, rebase.

sources.label: GitHub Docs — REST API endpoints for rules (orgs/rules)
sources.summary: Org-level ruleset endpoint (POST /orgs/{org}/rulesets) supports the same pull_request rule with allowed_merge_methods parameter, plus conditions.repository_name + ref_name (~DEFAULT_BRANCH). Confirms org-level enforceability across repos matched by repository_name.

sources.label: GitHub Docs — About merge methods on GitHub
sources.summary: Repo-level: enforce one merge method by only enabling the desired method (allow_squash_merge etc.). Note: 'Merge methods set on the repository that conflict with the merge method rule will prevent merging' — repo settings and ruleset merge rule interact; conflicts block merges.

sources.label: GitHub Docs — Creating rulesets for repositories in your organization (aggregation semantics)
sources.summary: Org-level rulesets aggregate with repo-level; result can only become MORE restrictive, never less. Org owners control org-level ruleset edits. Confirms org ruleset is the as-code org-wide enforcement surface.

subagent_reports.architecture_assessment: GitHub rulesets DO support merge-method restriction natively via the pull_request rule's allowed_merge_methods parameter, and this parameter is available at the ORG level (POST /orgs/{org}/rulesets), not just per-repo. The existing org-level 'Sharperflow App Protection' ruleset already carries a pull_request rule; squash-only can be enforced org-wide as-code by setting allowed_merge_methods:['squash'] on that rule. This makes the per-repo allow_squash_merge/allow_merge_commit/allow_rebase_merge settings unnecessary for ENFORCEMENT (the ruleset blocks non-squash merges regardless), though leaving merge_commit/rebase enabled on the repo creates a visible-but-blocked UI state and a conflict surface. non_fast_forward / linear history does NOT achieve squash-only: linear history forbids merge commits but still permits BOTH squash and rebase, so explicit allowed_merge_methods is required to exclude rebase.

validation.notes: Squash-only IS ruleset-enforceable at org level via pull_request.allowed_merge_methods=['squash']. Confirmed in official REST docs for both repos/rules and orgs/rules. Team plan supports org-level rulesets on private repos (rulesets GA for private repos on all plans incl. Team). No GHAS/CodeQL dependency.

subagent_reports.recommendation: Enforce squash-only as-code by adding allowed_merge_methods:['squash'] to the pull_request rule in the existing org-level 'Sharperflow App Protection' ruleset (option a). Then ALSO normalize repo settings (allow_merge_commit=false, allow_rebase_merge=false, allow_squash_merge=true) via PATCH /repos to remove dead/blocked merge buttons and avoid ruleset-vs-repo conflict — treat this as a secondary hygiene step in apply-ruleset.sh, not the primary enforcement. Do NOT rely on non_fast_forward for squash-only (it permits rebase too).
follow_ups: Verify Sharper-Flow org ruleset JSON includes a pull_request rule object to attach allowed_merge_methods to (gh api /orgs/Sharper-Flow/rulesets).
Add repo-settings normalization (squash-only merge buttons) to conformance checklist / apply script as hygiene, since repo allow_* flags cannot be set by an org ruleset and remain drift-prone.

subagent_reports.topic: Independent design validation: squash-only org-ruleset collision fix (5-tier)

sources.label: GitHub Docs — Available rules for rulesets
sources.summary: Confirms ruleset can require merge type merge/squash/rebase; 'if the repository has disabled a merge method and the ruleset required a different method, the merge will be blocked'; Strict='branch must be up to date', Loose=not required; Require-linear-history (NOT force-push) blocks merge commits and needs repo to allow squash OR rebase first; Block-force-pushes is a separate rule.

sources.label: GitHub Docs — About merge methods on GitHub
sources.summary: Squash uses fast-forward; rebase resembles fast-forward; default merge commit uses --no-ff. NOTE: 'Merge methods set on the repository that conflict with the merge method rule will prevent merging' — confirms ruleset-vs-repo blocked-merge hazard.

sources.label: GitHub Docs — REST endpoints for rules (rule-parameter schema)
sources.summary: pull_request.allowed_merge_methods: array of merge|squash|rebase, 'At least one option must be enabled.' non_fast_forward='Prevent users with push access from force pushing to refs.' required_linear_history='Prevent merge commits from being pushed to matching refs.' Two distinct rules.

sources.label: GitHub Changelog — Pull request merge method rule (Public Preview, 2024-12-04)
sources.summary: Merge-method rule available for rulesets at repository, ORGANIZATION, and enterprise level — confirms org-level applicability on the Team-plan org.

sources.label: Terraform integrations/github repository_ruleset schema
sources.summary: Corroborates: non_fast_forward='Prevent force pushing'; required_linear_history='Prevent merge commits'; pull_request.allowed_merge_methods Min:1 of merge|squash|rebase.

sources.label: Working-dir: scripts/apply-ruleset.sh
sources.summary: --no-release-bypass sets PAYLOAD_FILE=RULESET_FILE verbatim (L122-124) and PUTs via --input (L184); python transform (L125-155) runs ONLY with a bypass id and preserves all keys except bypass_actors. allowed_merge_methods cannot be stripped. Payload-passthrough claim VERIFIED.

sources.label: Working-dir: .github/workflows/self-test.yml
sources.summary: Validate-ruleset-JSON L68 asserts strict_required_status_checks_policy is True (WILL fail after Tier 1); L67 asserts ctxs==['Sharperflow CI Gate'] (unaffected); L62 bypass_actors==[] (unaffected). No allowed_merge_methods assertion yet (AC2 adds it).

sources.label: Working-dir: rulesets/sharperflow-app-protection.json
sources.summary: Current ruleset: pull_request rule WITHOUT allowed_merge_methods, required_status_checks strict=true, non_fast_forward present, NO required_linear_history. Merge-commit/rebase currently UNRESTRICTED; allowed_merge_methods:[squash] is the sole squash-only enforcer.

subagent_reports.architecture_assessment: VERDICT: CONCERNS. Core mechanism is sound and GitHub-doc-verified: org rulesets support pull_request.allowed_merge_methods (org/enterprise/repo, values merge|squash|rebase, >=1 required); strict=false removes the 'branch up to date' requirement that is the verified collision cause; apply-ruleset.sh --no-release-bypass passes the JSON verbatim so allowed_merge_methods cannot be stripped. Tiers 2-5 consistent with all 8 ACs / 7 constraints / 6 avoidances. THREE issues need correction before design sign-off: one factual rationale error (non_fast_forward mislabeled as merge-commit/linear-history guard), one under-weighted real merge-blocking hazard (repo squash must be enabled or squash-only ruleset hard-blocks merges), one sequencing fix tied to it. Not CONFLICT — the approach is valid; CONCERNS because the design doc contains a false mechanism statement and an under-specified repo-settings precondition.

validation.notes:
FINDINGS:

[CONCERN-1 | factual error, item 1] Design 'Verified mechanics' says 'non_fast_forward (linear history) forbids merge commits but NOT rebase'. WRONG per REST schema + Terraform + available-rules: non_fast_forward = force-push block ('Prevent force pushing to refs'); the merge-commit blocker is required_linear_history ('Prevent merge commits'), which is NOT in this ruleset. So 'keep non_fast_forward for the no-merge-commit guarantee' is false. FIX: state that the ruleset has no required_linear_history, so allowed_merge_methods:[squash] is the SOLE enforcer of squash-only (excludes both merge-commit AND rebase); non_fast_forward is retained only as the force-push block. Operational conclusion (need allowed_merge_methods to exclude rebase) is correct and actually stronger than the design realizes. Doc-correctness concern, not a mechanism failure.

[CONCERN-2 | real blocked-merge hazard, items 6&7] GitHub docs: 'Merge methods set on the repository that conflict with the merge method rule will prevent merging.' Once ruleset enforces allowed_merge_methods:[squash], BOTH repos MUST have allow_squash_merge=true or ALL merges hard-block. Design calls disabling repo allow_merge_commit 'cosmetic hygiene only' — wrong framing: ruleset restricts to the INTERSECTION; empty intersection (repo squash OFF) = hard block, not silent override. FIX: make repo allow_squash_merge=true a HARD PRECONDITION in AC1/AC7, verified before apply; treat repo allow_* normalization as REQUIRED not cosmetic; AC7 must assert allow_squash_merge=true explicitly.

[CONCERN-3 | sequencing, item 6] Design applies ruleset (step 3) before cross-repo (step 4). If a repo has squash disabled at apply time, squash-only ruleset wedges all merges — self-inflicted outage of the same class this change fixes. FIX: verify/normalize repo allow_squash_merge=true BEFORE the privileged apply, or gate the apply runbook on a pre-apply repo-settings check. Otherwise docs-first/privileged-apply/cross-repo-last order is sound.

[CONFIRM-4 | self-test, item 3] VERIFIED: self-test.yml L68 (strict is True) WILL fail after Tier 1; AC2 correctly updates it. L67 ctxs, L62 bypass_actors, target/enforcement assertions all UNAFFECTED. Only L68 breaks; AC2 also adds allowed_merge_methods assertion. No other assertion breaks — design covers it.

[CONFIRM-5 | payload risk, item 2] CLEARED. --no-release-bypass uses JSON verbatim as PUT --input; python bypass path skipped without bypass id and preserves all keys but bypass_actors even when run. allowed_merge_methods cannot be dropped. Claim accurate.

[NIT-6 | strict-off honesty, item 4] Residual-risk framing is HONEST and matches GitHub 'Loose' doc ('status checks may fail after merge if incompatible changes'). Agreement explicitly states the green-but-incompatible-pair risk and that oasdiff covers only backend->frontend OpenAPI. Optional: add one sentence that loose checks do NOT re-evaluate post-merge, making next-PR CI the explicit safety net.

[INTERNAL CONSISTENCY | item 5] All 8 ACs map to tiers (AC1/AC2->T1; AC3->T2/T3/T4 docs; AC4->T2 examples; AC5->T2; AC6->T5; AC7/AC8->verification). Constraints C1-C7 all respected (no GHEC, no paid SaaS, CI stays in GH cloud, privileged apply as handoff, cross-repo handoffs, escalation within rulesets, fail-on-high-signal). Avoidances DONT1-DONT6 all respected (no Temporal mutex, no merge_queue, promote not rebuild oasdiff, defer updater choice, no bypass actors, no Tufin action). No AC uncovered. Only internal-consistency defect is CONCERN-1's false statement contradicting the cited REST schema.

subagent_reports.recommendation: Proceed to design-gate AFTER three edits: (1) correct CONCERN-1 — non_fast_forward is the force-push block, NOT a merge-commit/linear-history guard; allowed_merge_methods:[squash] is the sole squash-only enforcer (no required_linear_history rule exists); cite REST schema. (2) CONCERN-2/3 — make repo allow_squash_merge=true a hard precondition verified BEFORE the privileged squash-only apply on both PokeEdge + PokeEdge-Web; reorder so repo merge-method normalization precedes apply; AC7 asserts allow_squash_merge=true. (3) NIT-6 — add the one-sentence loose-checks/next-PR-CI safety-net note. Payload-passthrough (item 2) and self-test consistency (item 3) verified correct as designed.
follow_ups: Backlog: bind required status check to GitHub Actions integration_id (ci-standard.md §6 optional hardening) to prevent context spoofing — out of scope here.
Execution: run apply-ruleset.sh --dry-run and diff composed payload vs rulesets/sharperflow-app-protection.json to empirically confirm allowed_merge_methods survives the PUT.
Pre-apply: check both repos via `gh api repos/{owner}/{repo} --jq .allow_squash_merge` (CONCERN-2 precondition).

wisdom_candidates.content: GitHub Markdown heading anchors: github.com renders files with github-slugger (Flet/github-slugger), whose core is value.toLowerCase().replace(specialRegex,'').replace(/ /g,'-') — it does NOT collapse runs. A heading with ' + ' or an em-dash ' — ' between spaces yields DOUBLE hyphens in the slug (e.g. 'strict-off + squash-only' -> 'strict-off--squash-only'). Many online guides (and the popular creachadair gist) describe run-collapsing to a single hyphen — that is WRONG for github.com file rendering. Verify anchors against the actual lib source, not prose summaries. The GitHub /markdown API does NOT emit heading id= anchors, so it can't be used to confirm slugs.

When reviewing intra-doc anchor consistency, generate heading slugs programmatically with the real github-slugger regex and diff against every markdown-link target ([text](#anchor) and [text](file.md#anchor)). Filter prose parentheses like '(below)'/'(2026-06-08)' so they aren't mistaken for anchor links. This catches double-hyphen slug breakage that visual review misses.

documents.proposal:
## Chosen direction: GitHub-native serialization + standardize existing contract gate + Dependabot equal-path

Native merge queue is unavailable (Team + private, verified). The collision loop is self-inflicted by `strict_required_status_checks_policy: true`. Fix it natively (strict-off + auto-merge + squash, all server-side, multi-machine-safe, bot-native). Discovery revealed the cross-repo oasdiff contract gate ALREADY EXISTS on the backend — so Tier 2 is standardization, not new build. User reversed the Renovate-only rule: support Renovate OR Dependabot as equal paths (future per-repo re-assessment).

## SCOPE
This change owns the org-wide merge/CI policy. Files/repos affected:
- **sharperflow-security-gates (this repo, primary):**
  - `rulesets/sharperflow-app-protection.json` — set `strict_required_status_checks_policy: false`; add squash-only merge-method enforcement (surface decided in design: ruleset rule vs repo settings).
  - `docs/ci-standard.md` — amend §6 (drop strict rationale → document strict-off + auto-merge + squash); amend §Dependency updates (Renovate→Renovate-OR-Dependabot equal path, supersede adoptRenovateOrgWide one-updater rule, carry forward tradeoffs); add canonical cross-repo OpenAPI contract-gate section (promote existing backend oasdiff pattern); add Tier-3 escalation playbook (Mergify or AI-PR-bot survey); update conformance checklist.
  - `examples/pokeedge-python/ci.yml`, `examples/pokeedge-web/ci.yml` — refresh to match live conformant pattern (currently stale: missing contract gate).
  - `scripts/apply-ruleset.sh` — verify/extend for merge-method rule if needed.
  - `self-test.yml` — update ruleset JSON assertions (strict now false; squash rule).
  - `README.md` — pointer updates.
- **Sharper-Flow/PokeEdge (cross-repo app edits):** remove stale `sonarcloud.yml` (retireSonarcloud drift); reconcile Dependabot state per new equal-path standard; confirm squash-only.
- **Sharper-Flow/PokeEdge-Web (cross-repo app edits):** ensure conformance to standardized contract gate; confirm squash-only + auto-merge.
- **Org ruleset apply:** privileged `apply-ruleset.sh` run (admin:org) — runbook/handoff step.

## Tier 1 — dissolve the collision loop ($0, the actual fix)
1. `strict_required_status_checks_policy: false` in ruleset JSON; apply via `apply-ruleset.sh --no-release-bypass` (keeps disk=live; fixes detected drift). Keep required check "Sharperflow CI Gate", keep `non_fast_forward`.
2. Enforce squash-only merge method (surface TBD in design).
3. Standardize `gh pr merge --squash --auto`; document auto-merge for both bot paths.
Effect: no up-to-date requirement → no rebase/CI-rerun churn; PRs land serially server-side at GitHub. Works single- and multi-machine.

## Tier 2 — standardize the EXISTING cross-repo contract gate ($0)
4. Promote the shipped backend pattern (archived ciContractGateRunContractTests, 2026-05-10): `check-api-compat.yml` reusable workflow runs oasdiff comparing backend `openapi.json` vs frontend's committed `docs/openapi.json` baseline; `contract-gate` job path-filtered on spec changes; required check. Document oasdiff rationale (chosen for breaking-change detection on the OpenAPI surface). Replicate/document as canonical in ci-standard.md so all repos converge. Audit web side conformance; design makes final tool call (keep oasdiff vs alternative).

## Tier 3 — documented escalation (no build now)
5. Escalation trigger doc + candidates: Mergify free tier OR another AI-enabled PR-merge bot (surveyed at doc time). Verified caveats recorded: bot-author free-tier billing must be confirmed; bot-PR queueing config + end-to-end test required; keep any tool WITHIN rulesets (never bypass actor on a security-gates org).

## Tier 4 — Dependabot equal-path reconcile (user reversal)
6. Supersede adoptRenovateOrgWide's "Renovate-only, one updater" rule. Amend ci-standard.md to support Renovate OR Dependabot as equal paths, one-updater-PER-ECOSYSTEM-per-repo (verified hard rule). Document Dependabot parity: GA `cooldown` (7d default, matches Renovate minimumReleaseAge; security fixes exempt on both), `--auto --squash` auto-merge workflow, ecosystem support (uv/bun/pnpm/actions/docker) with weaker spots flagged (bun min-version, action SHA-comment edge cases, docker no-cooldown). Record inputs for the FUTURE per-repo re-assessment.

## Tier 5 — fix live drift (in this change, per user)
7. Remove stale `sonarcloud.yml` from PokeEdge (retireSonarcloud left it). Reconcile PokeEdge Dependabot state to conform to the new equal-path standard. Confirm both repos squash-only + allow_auto_merge (already true).

## Residual risk (accepted)
Strict OFF accepts a rare green-but-incompatible PR pair breaking main, caught by next PR's CI. The oasdiff cross-repo gate closes the highest-value coupling. Reassess if break frequency rises (→ Tier 3 escalation).

## Out of scope
- GitHub Enterprise Cloud upgrade. Agent-side Temporal merge mutex (wrong-fit: local hardware tax, cannot govern bot PRs). Native merge_queue rule (impossible on plan). Third-party queue ADOPTION (documented as escalation only). The future Renovate-vs-Dependabot per-repo decision (this change enables both; the choice is deferred).

## Verified facts (discovery)
- Org plan=team, both repos private → native merge queue impossible (422s were correct).
- Live ruleset id 17335746 strict=true (collision cause); matches disk.
- Both repos: allow_auto_merge=true, all 3 merge methods allowed, delete_branch_on_merge=true, default branch main.
- Backend already: merge_group:, Sharperflow CI Gate summary, oasdiff contract-gate, Dependabot --auto --squash auto-merge, OpenAPI Drift Check; stale sonarcloud.yml present.
- Dependabot now has GA cooldown (parity with Renovate); equal-path viable.
documents.problemStatement:
Recent org-wide security/CI rollout left a merge-collision problem: many AI agents (and future multi-machine + Renovate/Dependabot bots) open PRs near-simultaneously and thrash.

VERIFIED ROOT CAUSE (not the tickets' premise):
- Org Sharper-Flow is GitHub **Team plan**, repos PokeEdge + PokeEdge-Web are **private** (confirmed via `gh api orgs/Sharper-Flow` → plan team; both repos private:true). GitHub-native merge queue is therefore IMPOSSIBLE (requires Enterprise Cloud for private repos). The two source tickets' "enable merge queue" premise is infeasible; the 422 errors were correct rejections, not bugs.
- The collision loop is SELF-INFLICTED by `strict_required_status_checks_policy: true` ("branch must be up to date") in the org ruleset (live ruleset id 17335746, confirmed strict=true; matches committed rulesets/sharperflow-app-protection.json line 31). Ticket 1's claim that strict was "flipped off" is stale/wrong — strict is currently ON.
- This is actually TWO problems conflated: (a) the rebase/CI-rerun collision loop, caused by strict=true; (b) cross-repo OpenAPI drift (backend spec change breaks a green frontend PR) — which NO merge queue addresses.

CONSTRAINTS:
- Rapid dev, mostly single machine today; must scale to multiple machines submitting PRs/pushes in future.
- Must work natively with Renovate OR Dependabot (bot-authored PRs are first-class).
- No GitHub Enterprise Cloud upgrade (cost rejected). No paid SaaS as the starting move.
- Keep CI in GitHub's free cloud — do not pull verification onto local hardware (rules out agent-side Temporal merge mutex as primary: it taxes local compute and cannot govern bot PRs that never touch ADV).
- This repo (sharperflow-security-gates) is the protection-as-code source of truth; fixes belong here (org ruleset JSON + apply-ruleset.sh), not UI clicking (avoids the code/live drift already detected).
documents.agreement:
# Agreement — fixMergeCollisionStrategy

## Objectives
1. Dissolve the PR merge-collision loop by removing its verified cause (org ruleset strict=true): set strict-off + squash-only + native auto-merge, server-side at GitHub (works single- and multi-machine, Renovate- and Dependabot-native).
2. Standardize the existing (already-shipped on backend) oasdiff cross-repo OpenAPI breaking-change contract gate org-wide, so backend→frontend API drift is caught pre-merge (the one coupling no merge queue addresses).
3. Document a Tier-3 escalation path (Mergify free tier OR another AI-enabled PR-merge bot) for if collisions persist after strict-off — documentation only, no adoption.
4. Reconcile ci-standard.md to support Renovate OR Dependabot as equal updater paths (supersedes adoptRenovateOrgWide "Renovate-only, one updater" rule per explicit user reversal); enforce one-updater-PER-ECOSYSTEM-per-repo; record inputs for a future per-repo re-assessment.
5. Fix live drift discovered during discovery: remove stale sonarcloud.yml from PokeEdge (retireSonarcloud leftover); reconcile PokeEdge Dependabot state to the new equal-path standard.

## Acceptance Criteria
- **AC1** Org ruleset (rulesets/sharperflow-app-protection.json): strict_required_status_checks_policy=false; squash-only merge enforced (surface decided in design — ruleset merge-method rule vs repo settings); non_fast_forward + required check "Sharperflow CI Gate" retained; applied via apply-ruleset.sh --no-release-bypass; disk=live (drift resolved).
- **AC2** self-test.yml ruleset-JSON assertions updated to match (strict now false; squash enforcement asserted).
- **AC3** docs/ci-standard.md: §6 amended (strict-off + auto-merge + squash rationale replaces strict rationale); §Dependency-updates amended to Renovate-OR-Dependabot equal path (supersession of adoptRenovateOrgWide documented, carried-forward tradeoffs noted, one-updater-per-ecosystem-per-repo hard rule); new canonical cross-repo OpenAPI contract-gate section (oasdiff pattern promoted); Tier-3 escalation playbook section; conformance checklist updated.
- **AC4** examples/pokeedge-python/ci.yml + examples/pokeedge-web/ci.yml refreshed to match live conformant pattern (currently stale — missing contract gate).
- **AC5** oasdiff standardized via CLI or the NEW oasdiff/oasdiff-action ONLY (never deprecated Tufin/oasdiff-action); gate on --fail-on ERR (high-signal first); version-pinned + Renovate-bumpable.
- **AC6** Sharper-Flow/PokeEdge: stale sonarcloud.yml removed; Dependabot state conformant to new equal-path standard; squash-only confirmed.
- **AC7** Both PokeEdge + PokeEdge-Web verified: allow_auto_merge on, squash-only, required "Sharperflow CI Gate" intact, default branch main.
- **AC8** README.md pointers updated where standard sections moved/changed.

## Constraints
- **C1** No GitHub Enterprise Cloud upgrade.
- **C2** No new paid SaaS adoption (Tier-3 escalation is documentation only).
- **C3** Keep CI execution in GitHub's free cloud — do not move verification onto local hardware.
- **C4** Org-ruleset apply is a privileged operation (admin:org / GitHub App Administration:write) — a runbook/handoff step, not an automated CI mutation.
- **C5** Cross-repo app edits (PokeEdge sonarcloud/dependabot) are privileged/cross-project — sequence as handoffs where this session cannot directly push.
- **C6** Keep any escalation tool operating WITHIN rulesets — never a bypass actor on a security-gates org.
- **C7** Maintain "fail only on high-signal" posture (oasdiff --fail-on ERR first).

## Avoidances
- **DONT1** Do not adopt/build agent-side Temporal merge mutex (local hardware tax; cannot govern bot PRs).
- **DONT2** Do not attempt native merge_queue rule (impossible on Team+private; 422s were correct rejections).
- **DONT3** Do not build a new oasdiff gate from scratch — promote/standardize the existing backend pattern.
- **DONT4** Do not make the future Renovate-vs-Dependabot per-repo CHOICE in this change — enable both, defer the decision.
- **DONT5** Do not weaken the ruleset with bypass actors to accommodate any tool.
- **DONT6** Do not use the deprecated Tufin/oasdiff-action.

## Accepted residual risk
Strict-off allows a rare green-but-logically-incompatible PR pair to merge and break main, caught by the next PR's CI. The oasdiff cross-repo gate closes the highest-value coupling (backend→frontend API). Reassess and escalate (Tier 3) if break frequency rises.

## Out of scope
GHEC upgrade; agent-side Temporal mutex; native merge_queue; third-party queue ADOPTION (doc only); the future per-repo Renovate-vs-Dependabot decision (enabled here, decided later).

## Sign-Off
Approved by user ("approve") at agreement checkpoint, 2026-06-08.

# Design — fixMergeCollisionStrategy

(v2 — incorporates independent design-validator findings C-1, C-2, C-3.)

## Architecture posture
This repo (sharperflow-security-gates) is the protection-as-code + CI-standard source of truth. All policy changes land here as code/docs; the org ruleset is applied via the existing privileged script; cross-repo app edits (PokeEdge) are sequenced as handoffs. Every external-surface decision below is GitHub-doc-verified (P34/P36).

## Verified mechanics (design-load-bearing)
- **Native merge queue impossible** (Team+private). Not pursued.
- **Squash-only is ORG-RULESET enforceable**: `pull_request` rule param `allowed_merge_methods: ["squash"]` (REST repos/orgs rules; org-level since GitHub changelog 2024-12-04). Lowercase enum `merge|squash|rebase`, ≥1 required. → Tier 1 stays fully as-code in the existing org ruleset.
- **CORRECTED (validator C-1): `non_fast_forward` ONLY blocks force-pushes — it does NOT forbid merge commits.** The merge-commit blocker would be `required_linear_history`, which is NOT in this ruleset. Therefore `allowed_merge_methods: ["squash"]` is the SOLE squash-only enforcer — it alone excludes BOTH merge-commit AND rebase. Keep `non_fast_forward` only as the force-push guard. (Earlier rationale claiming non_fast_forward gives the no-merge-commit guarantee was false; operational conclusion — squash-only via allowed_merge_methods — is unchanged and correct.)
- **HARD PRECONDITION (validator C-2/C-3): repo `allow_squash_merge` MUST be true on BOTH repos BEFORE applying the squash-only ruleset.** GitHub: "Merge methods set on the repository that conflict with the merge method rule will prevent merging." A squash-only ruleset against a repo with squash disabled = empty intersection = ALL merges hard-block (a self-inflicted outage of the same class this change fixes). Both repos currently have allow_squash_merge=true (verified in discovery), so safe — but the apply runbook MUST gate on a pre-apply check. Normalizing repo allow_merge_commit/allow_rebase → false is recommended hygiene (removes dead/blocked merge buttons), NOT load-bearing; allow_squash_merge=true IS load-bearing.
- **strict=false** removes the "branch up to date" requirement (GitHub "Loose" mode). Loose checks are NOT re-evaluated post-merge → the NEXT PR's CI is the explicit safety net for a green-but-stale pair.
- **oasdiff**: use CLI (as PokeEdge does) or NEW oasdiff/oasdiff-action; never deprecated Tufin action; `--fail-on ERR`; version-pinned + Renovate-bumped.
- **Dependabot equal-path**: GA `cooldown` (default-days 7 ≈ Renovate minimumReleaseAge; security fixes exempt on both); `--auto --squash` auto-merge workflow w/ actor guard + contents:write+pull-requests:write + Dependabot secrets; one-updater-PER-ECOSYSTEM-per-repo (hard rule).

## Implementation strategy by tier

### Tier 0 — PRE-APPLY GUARD (new, from validator C-2/C-3) [AC7]
- BEFORE applying the ruleset: verify `allow_squash_merge=true` on BOTH PokeEdge + PokeEdge-Web (gh api). If false anywhere, normalize first (PATCH allow_squash_merge=true) — otherwise the squash-only ruleset hard-blocks all merges. Recommended same-step hygiene: set allow_merge_commit=false + allow_rebase_merge=false to remove dead buttons. Add this as an explicit gate in the apply runbook.

### Tier 1 — collision-loop fix (org ruleset as-code) [AC1, AC2]
1. Edit `rulesets/sharperflow-app-protection.json`:
   - required_status_checks rule: `strict_required_status_checks_policy: false` (was true).
   - pull_request rule: add `allowed_merge_methods: ["squash"]` (keep the full existing param set — API requires all pull_request sub-fields).
   - Keep non_fast_forward (force-push guard), required check "Sharperflow CI Gate", bypass_actors:[].
2. Update `.github/workflows/self-test.yml` JSON assertions: change the hardcoded `strict ... is True` assertion (~L68) → assert False; ADD assertion that the pull_request rule's allowed_merge_methods == ["squash"]. (Validator confirmed only L68 breaks; L67 ctxs / L62 bypass_actors / target / enforcement assertions unaffected.)
3. Apply: Tier-0 guard → `scripts/apply-ruleset.sh --dry-run` preview → `scripts/apply-ruleset.sh --no-release-bypass` (privileged, admin:org — runbook/handoff). Validator confirmed apply-ruleset.sh uses the JSON verbatim as PUT --input on the --no-release-bypass path; allowed_merge_methods cannot be stripped. No script edit needed.

### Tier 2 — standardize existing oasdiff contract gate (doc-first) [AC3 contract section, AC4, AC5]
- Canonical pattern already in PokeEdge: `check-api-compat.yml` (reusable workflow_call) runs oasdiff comparing backend openapi.json vs frontend's committed docs/openapi.json baseline; `contract-gate` job path-filters on openapi.json, required via "Sharperflow CI Gate".
- Standardization = DOCUMENT as canonical in ci-standard.md (dedicated "Cross-repo API contract gate" section): baseline model (frontend committed spec = built-against), `--fail-on ERR`, CLI/new-action-only + version-pin, path-filter + skip=success, required-check rollup; reference PokeEdge impl as reference implementation.
- Refresh examples/ to show the contract-gate job in the canonical CI template.
- Audit PokeEdge-Web conformance (it has "Generated API Drift Check"/"Backend Contract Sync Check"); reconcile naming/behavior to the standard in docs; fix only if cheap+adjacent (campsite), else surface follow-up.
- DESIGN DECISION (final): keep oasdiff (validated). Do NOT duplicate check-api-compat.yml into THIS repo unless web genuinely needs cross-repo consumption — prefer doc + reference impl. Execution audits whether a shared reusable workflow adds value vs doc-only.

### Tier 3 — escalation playbook (doc only) [AC3 escalation section]
- New ci-standard.md subsection: "If collisions persist after strict-off" → trigger criteria + candidates (Mergify free OR another AI-enabled PR-merge bot). Caveats: bot-author free-tier billing must be confirmed; bot-PR queueing config + end-to-end test required; tool MUST operate within rulesets (never bypass actor). No adoption.

### Tier 4 — Dependabot equal-path reconcile [AC3 dependency-updates section]
- Amend ci-standard.md §Dependency updates: "Renovate, NOT Dependabot — one updater" → "Renovate OR Dependabot — one updater PER ECOSYSTEM per repo". Document supersession of adoptRenovateOrgWide (date + reason: user reversal; future per-repo re-assessment). Carry forward tradeoffs (duplicate-PR/lockfile risk if mixed per ecosystem; Renovate bun maturity edge; Dependabot action SHA-comment edge cases; Dependabot docker no-cooldown). Include canonical Dependabot `--auto --squash` workflow + `cooldown` config block. List re-assessment inputs.

### Tier 5 — fix live drift [AC6]
- PokeEdge (cross-repo handoff PRs): remove stale `.github/workflows/sonarcloud.yml` (retireSonarcloud leftover); reconcile Dependabot state to the new standard (already runs Dependabot auto-merge — confirm conformance; no removal needed now that Dependabot is allowed). Confirm squash effective once ruleset applies (depends on Tier-0 guard).
- Privileged cross-project edits — sequence as handoff steps (open PRs via gh where possible; org-owner merges/applies).

### Verification [AC7, AC8]
- self-test actionlint + ruleset-JSON assertions green.
- Live post-apply: ruleset strict=false + allowed_merge_methods=["squash"]; both repos allow_auto_merge on, allow_squash_merge=true, required check intact, default branch main.
- README pointer updates.

## Sequencing / dependencies (REVISED per validator C-3)
1. Ruleset JSON + self-test assertions (this repo) → 2. ci-standard.md amendments + examples refresh (this repo) → 3. **Tier-0 pre-apply guard: verify/normalize repo allow_squash_merge=true on both repos** → 4. apply ruleset (privileged handoff, dry-run first) → 5. cross-repo drift PRs (PokeEdge) → 6. live verification.
Docs (this repo) are independent and land first. The Tier-0 guard MUST precede the privileged apply.

## Risks / mitigations
- strict=false is privileged + feels irreversible: dry-run preview first; rollback = flip strict true + re-apply.
- **Squash-only-vs-repo-settings hard-block (validator C-2): mitigated by the Tier-0 precondition guard — never apply before confirming allow_squash_merge=true on both repos.**
- Cross-repo edits may exceed this session's push scope: sequence as explicit handoffs; do not bypass worktree/permission isolation.
- oasdiff org-wide doc must not duplicate PokeEdge's working workflow: doc + reference impl preferred.
- LBP: keep ruleset, standard doc, examples, self-test assertions mutually consistent (standard is normative; YAML/JSON wins on conflict per ci-standard.md precedence).

## Design-validator disposition
Independent validator verdict: CONCERNS (approach valid). C-1 (false non_fast_forward rationale) FIXED above. C-2/C-3 (squash-precondition + sequencing) FIXED via new Tier-0 guard + revised sequencing + AC7 assertion of allow_squash_merge=true. Payload-pass-through (item 5) and self-test L68 (item 4) confirmed correct as designed. All 8 ACs / 7 constraints / 6 avoidances covered.

# Executive Summary — fixMergeCollisionStrategy

## What it solves
PR merge-collision loops on a small-seat org running many concurrent AI-agent/bot PRs. The verified root cause was **not** a missing merge queue — it was `strict_required_status_checks_policy: true` ("branch must be up to date") in the org ruleset, which forced endless rebase + CI-rerun churn as PRs raced.

## Key finding (reframed the whole ask)
The two source tickets assumed GitHub merge queue could be enabled. **It cannot** on this account (Team plan + private repos require GitHub Enterprise Cloud for merge queue — confirmed via `gh api`: plan=team, both repos private). The 422 errors the prior agents hit were correct rejections, not bugs. With GHEC rejected on cost, the fix is built from plan-compatible GitHub primitives.

## What was delivered
**Tier 1 — collision-loop fix (live):** org ruleset `strict=false` + `allowed_merge_methods:["squash"]`. PRs now merge serially via native auto-merge (`gh pr merge --squash --auto`) on a green `Sharperflow CI Gate` — server-side, no rebase churn, identical single- or multi-machine, Renovate/Dependabot-native. Applied live to ruleset 17335746; disk==live verified.

**Tier 2 — cross-repo contract gate (standardized):** the backend↔frontend OpenAPI drift that no merge serialization can catch is closed by **oasdiff** breaking-change detection (already shipped on PokeEdge; validated as the de-facto best-practice tool). Documented as canonical in ci-standard.md (consumer-committed-spec baseline model, `--fail-on ERR`, CLI/new-action only — never the deprecated Tufin action, >1MB Git-Blobs-API gotcha). Both example CIs refreshed to show the producer (oasdiff) and consumer (sync) halves.

**Tier 3 — escalation playbook (documented, not adopted):** if collisions persist, Mergify free tier or another AI-PR-bot — with verified caveats (bot-author billing, bot-PR config, must stay within rulesets).

**Tier 4 — Dependabot equal-path:** reversed the 2-day-old Renovate-only rule per explicit user decision. ci-standard.md now supports Renovate OR Dependabot (one updater per ecosystem per repo), with Dependabot's GA cooldown + `--auto --squash` workflow documented and a re-assessment-inputs table for the future per-repo choice.

**Tier 5 — live drift fix:** PokeEdge PR #347 opened removing the orphaned `sonarcloud.yml` (Sonar was retired org-wide), with squash auto-merge armed — itself a live demonstration of the new policy.

## Verification
- Local: actionlint clean, shellcheck clean, self-test ruleset-JSON assertions green (now assert strict-off + squash-only).
- Live: ruleset 17335746 confirmed strict=false + methods=["squash"] + bypass=[]; both repos squash-only + auto-merge on.
- Contract: 21/21 items pass/respected (8 ACs, 7 constraints, 6 avoidances), 0 failing.
- Independent design validator caught + we fixed: the false "non_fast_forward blocks merge commits" rationale (squash-only comes solely from allowed_merge_methods) and the squash-precondition hard-block hazard (pre-apply guard added).

## Design integrity
- No GHEC, no paid SaaS, no agent-side Temporal mutex (rejected: local-hardware tax + can't govern bot PRs), no native merge_queue (impossible on plan), no bypass actors (security-gates org keeps `bypass_actors: []`).
- Accepted residual risk: strict-off lets a rare green-but-incompatible PR pair break main, caught by the next PR's CI; the oasdiff gate closes the highest-value (cross-repo API) coupling.

## Follow-ups
- ag-DJe50eGl: clean residual SonarCloud doc references in PokeEdge (docs-only, out of this change's workflow-removal scope).
- Future: per-repo Renovate-vs-Dependabot re-assessment (enabled here, deferred by design).
